State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

May 14, 1976

Ms. Helen Nash
P.O. Box 331
Carmel, California 93921

Dear Ms. Nash:

Thank you for your letter of March 8, 1976 concerning the
mass mailing provision of the Political Reform Act. I
delayed responding to your letter until the Commission
adopted a regulation which answers your question.

The Commission has stated in Regulation 18225 that the
expense of your newsletter, if it conforms to the conditions
outlined in (4) (C) (see attached), would not be considered
an expenditure for the purpose of reporting under the law.
Because the newsletter is not a reportable expenditure, it
would also not be considered a mass mailing subject to the
requirements outlined in Section 84305 which states:

"...a copy of every mass mailing in support of
or in opposition to a state candidate or state
measure shall be sent to the Commission."

I hope this answers any question you may have regarding your
obligation under the law. If you desire additional informa-
tion, please do not hesitate to let me know.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

RSK:bw

Enclosure

bcc: Nan
     Jeanne

Nov 17, 1977

Mr. Polan Nash
P.O. Box 331
Carmel, California 93921

Dear Mr. Nash:

Thank you for your letter of Nov. 3, 1977 concerning the
mass mailing provision of the Political Reform Act. I
have enclosed a copy of our interpretation of the FEC
adopted a resolution which answers your question.

The Commission has stated in Regulation 18035 that the
expenses of your newsletter is it conforms to the conditions
outlined in Section [illegible] which would not be considered
an expenditure for the purpose of reporting under
because the newsletter is not a removable expenditure, it
would also not be considered a mass mailing subject to the
requirements outlined in Section 84305 which states:

"...a copy of every mass mailing in support of
or in opposition to a state candidate or state
measure shall be sent to the Commission."

I hope this answers any question you may have regarding your
obligation under the law. If you desire additional information, please do not hesitate to let me know.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano Hutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

May 11, 1976

Mr. Helen Nash
P.O. Box 891
Carmel, California 93921

Dear Ms. Nash:

Please forgive the delay in responding to your letter of March 9,
requesting information concerning the mass mailing provision of the
Political Reform Act.

I have delayed responding to you until the Commission adopted a
regulation which outlined exactly what is considered an expenditure
under the law and whether or not the newsletter, as a mass mailing,
is considered a reportable expenditure.

The regulation (Proposed Regulation §12225) was adopted on
April 30, 1976 and states:

An organization formed or existing primarily for political
purposes including, but not limited to a political action
committee established by any membership organization, labor
union or corporation;

"Expenditure" includes any monetary or non-monetary payment
made by any person, other than those persons or organizations
described in subsection (b), that is used for communications
which expressly advocate the nomination, election or defeat
of a clearly identified candidate or candidate, or the
qualification, passage or defeat of a clearly identified
ballot measure.

Newsletters which include statements of support or opposition to
state candidates must be sent to the Fair Political Practices
Commission when two hundred (200) or more are mailed. (Section
82041.5 and 64305)

I hope this answers your question. If you desire additional infor-
mation, please do not hesitate to write me.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

[illegible]

March 3, 1976
Box 331
Carrol, Ca. 92921


Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance & Analysis Div.
1100 Kay St.
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Gentlemen:

We would like to know if your regulations concerning
mass mailing applies to SPARKS, a long-established
Democratic Women's Club newsletter. Are we required
to mail you copies?

We support all Democratic candidates.

Sincerely,

[illegible] Haedt

[illegible] - Journal + [illegible]
TED.
